DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KEVIN LEE O TARKINGTON, O'NEIL LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-777-5501 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING -The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COMAC INVESTMENTS, INC.'S General Demurrer to the Complaint on the basis of the statute of limitations is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to plead facts showing that Defendant had to have been aware of the alleged construction defects and willfully ignored them. (See California Code of Civil Procedure 337.15(f).) Plaintiff's boilerplate legal conclusion that the defects were the product of willful misconduct is not sufficient to save the claim from the 10-year Statute of Limitations. Plaintiff must plead facts, not legal conclusions. (See, e.g., Doe v. City of Los Angeles (2007) 42 Cal.4th 531, 551 at fn. 5 (complaint must plead facts, not legal conclusions, and it is insufficient to bring a claim outside a statute of limitations to merely "assert boilerplate allegations that defendants" had actual knowledge without alleging the facts which "lead[s] [the plaintiff] to believe that the allegations are true").) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
